title: Campaign Monitor vs Constant Contact (2025): Which Is Better?
meta_description: Compare Campaign Monitor and Constant Contact. See pricing, features, ratings, and which email marketing platform fits your business best.
h1: Campaign Monitor vs Constant Contact: Detailed Comparison
intro: Campaign Monitor and Constant Contact are both established email marketing platforms with different strengths. Campaign Monitor excels for agencies managing multiple brands, while Constant Contact is better suited for nonprofits and local businesses. Understanding their key differences will help you choose the right tool for your marketing needs.
choose_a_if: You manage multiple brands or client accounts and need multi-brand management capabilities
You want advanced automation features like journey designer and smart segments
You prioritize higher customer ratings and reliability reviews
choose_b_if: You run a nonprofit, local business, or e-commerce store needing SMS and social ads
You want integrated event management and list-building tools
You prefer a platform with broader marketing features beyond email

faq.q: Is Campaign Monitor better than Constant Contact?
faq.a: Campaign Monitor scores higher on review platforms (4.1 vs 4.0 on G2) and is better for agencies managing multiple brands. However, Constant Contact offers more diverse features like SMS, social ads, and e-commerce tools, making it better for local businesses and nonprofits. The choice depends on your specific needs rather than overall superiority.

faq.q: Which is cheaper, Campaign Monitor or Constant Contact?
faq.a: Pricing is nearly identical, with Campaign Monitor starting at $11/month and Constant Contact at $12/month. Both platforms have no free tier. The actual cost difference will depend on your subscriber count and feature requirements at higher pricing tiers.

faq.q: Can I switch from Constant Contact to Campaign Monitor?
faq.a: Yes, you can migrate your email lists and campaigns between platforms. Most email marketing tools support CSV exports of your contact lists, which can be imported into Campaign Monitor. However, you may need to rebuild automation workflows and customize templates for the new platform.
